assertions.text: The memo includes a clear one-sentence decision statement naming what's being decided and by when (tomorrow)
assertions.evidence: 'Decide by April 2 whether to (A) build SSO natively in H2, (B) adopt WorkOS or equivalent third-party auth, or (C) defer the three enterprise deals to Q1.'

assertions.text: The memo covers all 3 options: build, buy (WorkOS or equivalent), and delay
assertions.evidence: Options A, B, C all present with implications and what-to-believe conditions.

assertions.text: The memo explicitly separates customer value from internal convenience
assertions.evidence: Tradeoffs note: 'From the customer's perspective, Options A and B are functionally equivalent. The difference is entirely internal. Option C is the only one that's worse for the customer.'

assertions.text: The memo has a section naming specific unknowns
assertions.evidence: 'What We Don't Know' section with 5 specific unknowns.

assertions.text: The memo gives a clear recommendation (not hedged into 'it depends' without conditions)
assertions.evidence: 'Option B — Buy via WorkOS (or equivalent), targeting a close within 2 weeks.' With conditional to Option A.

assertions.text: The memo includes a risk table with likelihood, severity, and mitigation columns
assertions.evidence: Risk table with Risk | Likelihood | Severity | Mitigation, 5 rows.

assertions.text: The memo explicitly addresses whether cross-functional approvals are required (either names the functions that must be involved, or explicitly states no approvals are needed)
assertions.evidence: No 'Approvals Required' section. Security/Finance/Legal/Procurement implications of WorkOS vendor onboarding not surfaced.

assertions.text: The memo identifies Security and/or Finance as requiring approval, given the third-party vendor onboarding (WorkOS) and recurring spend
assertions.evidence: No approvals section. WorkOS vendor security review and Finance sign-off not mentioned.

assertions.text: The memo uses an appendix to offload detailed supporting content (e.g., cost breakdown, full option comparison), keeping the main body scannable
assertions.evidence: No appendix. Detailed tradeoffs table and financial comparison are in the main body. An appendix for the financial model would have kept the body more concise.
